title: Newness Glass Measuring Cup with Handle, 1000 ML (1 Liter, 4 Cup) Measuring Cup with Three Scales (OZ, Cup, ML/CC) and V-Shaped Spout, Measuring Beaker for Kitchen or Restaurant, Easy to Read
description:
About this item
Efficient Effect: Measuring cup with multiple measurements (cup, ounce and milliliter), easy to read, suitable for cooking, meet your daily needs
Compatibility: Made of high borosilicate glass, lightweight, apply to microwave, electric ceramic stove, oven and refrigerator, withstand Temperature Range: -4 to 302°F (-20° to 150°C)
Excellent Design: Wide mouth design, easy to clean, V-shaped spout without spilling, glass handle, comfortable grip
Recommendation: Hand wash method preferred, avoid washer potentially affecting marking/prevent dry-burning on heating, and ensure liquid in beaker
Comfortable and Lightweight Design: The measuring cup crafted from high borosilicate glass is incredibly lightweight. This not only makes it a breeze to handle but also significantly reduces the strain on your wrists. It's superbly suited for various kitchen tasks, whether you're whipping up a delicious meal, brewing a cup of coffee, or indulging in some baking
reviews_5s:
The glass measuring cup is better than the plastic. Lasts longer than plastic ones. Easy to use and see writing for measurements.So something’s like butter have to be melted for a recipe and this is a trusty glass measuring cup. Perfect for my needs. It has a handle for easy taking in and out of the microwave.The uses are endless with the beaker-nice handle and clear lines for measuring-it is a bit lightweight so I choose to clean by hand after using-can be used for either hot or cold applications-and a stick blender fits the circumference with plenty of room to spare-I recommend!This cup helps me in so many ways else you measure stuff and it’s very good and helpful. Highly recommendedI dislike gooseneck kettles and other things because I have NO counter space for anything else. But measuring cups don't pour right for my pour over coffee. This is perfect. The water flows in a small stream out of the beak and that's exactly what I needed. I use 14oz of water and that's exactly what I get with this!Love that it's not tall & eggs or whatever can be whipped in it easier. Not thick so be careful.
But I have ordered lot of these so I'm familiar with being thinner.
I never wash in my new dishwasher. It's older home I'm 69 & put in dishwasher recently with renovations. Love them all.Purchase it in June and have been using it daily to heat up water in my microwave for four and a half minutes and it has worked flawlessly.Just tried this out today. My old glass measuring cup would dribble liquid down the side when I stopped pouring. This one did not drip or side dribble at all. Great for me when decanting gee butter from the liquid layer below. I melted the butting in this cup on the stove top burner. I had the burner on L then 1 for an hour ...butter melted, but handle was not too hot to grab and pour. Will use it for gravy oil separation next.purchased two sizes and really like the look and feel.I was surprised at how svelte this cup is, yet it is strong enough to be kept outside where I use it to measure and dispense the jacuzzi chemicals. Certainly better than plastic measuring cups that break down in the elements. This item would do well in the kitchen where it is intended to be used! 🤔
reviews_1s:
I loved this glass measuring cup until it broke (see photo) after using it no more than 6 times within two months. I could tell it was fragile, so I hand washed it only, never putting it in the dishwasher. Very disappointed.I really liked this even though it appeared to b fragile glass. I liked the flat even on the way up edges and the different measuring units as I have the other kind . The sellers is correct this is much easier to pore without any spill back up the glass onto ur hand or what not . ......
Unfortunately it broke on its first HAND wash with a soft goam sponge made to clean glasses. Light pressure I was being very gentle and holding it on sides bottom. Luckily it stopped at major cracking and didn't actually break in my hand and cut me . That would have sucked . But now after one use it's useless, lol.
I don't have the time to use things as quickly as I would like, being super busy like most of us . So now my return window is closed 3 days ago and I am just out of time here as they say . I don't really like leaving negative reviews unless it's something that I think other ppl can be saved some $ or hassle on . This qualifies . Don't waste your money
Seller, maybe work on making it a bit sturdier I guess . 🤷‍♂️
Pic included if anyone wants to check it out .
Hope this helped ✌🏻🖖🏻As the title says…when this breaks it shatteres into almost a dust and that is not what you want with pets and kids around.I returned this, the measurements aren’t accurate and the pour spout is a wildly flawed design that drips liquid down the side of the measuring cup as much as it pours where you’re aiming for.
I can’t think of any way this is useful as a kitchen or other measuring tool. Maybe it could be useful as a flower vaseInaccurate graduations. 4 cup line is same as 1000ml line. Also shows 32oz less than 4 cups.I've bought two of these now in the last six month. The first one lasted about four months...and then the bottom shattered one day when I put it down on my kitchen counter. The second one lasted a little over a month...just long enough for the return window to close. The bottom on it shattered too while pouring lime juice into it.

The durability on these is terrible. Do not buy. The bottoms are too fragile and thin. And yes, I only hand washed mine.

Edit: To the seller's credit, they saw this review and refunded my money. So while I can't change my evaluation of their product at this time, I can at least commend and praise their customer service.I tried using this to make baby formula, which requires precise measurements for the water-to-powder ratio to be just right. To ensure accuracy, I put the measuring cup on a scale (and set it to 0 ml) before I poured the hot water into the glass. When the scale said 360ml, the water was above the 400ml marker on the glass. I double checked and poured that water into another measuring cup and it was in fact 360ml like the scale said, but not the Newness measuring glass. Seems like a “measuring cup” should, ya know, measure accurately. Returning!Perfect size and shape for making my pour over coffee. After 4 months of use, I was washing it in the sink with a soft sponge and a large hole broke out of the bottom. Extremely disappointed. I’d say this is much to fragile for everyday use.I like this glass when I first got it, and I was excited to have a better measuring cup. But the first time I microwaved some water in the cup and poured from it, it started creaking as if it was thin ice that was about to break. Glass that was about to shatter or even explode. Very distinct sound. Very scary.

I will be returning it.Was so happy when I got this, the first on got a crack in the bottom (not sure how) so I ordered another one. Well guess what? Same crack in the bottom edge as the first one. Want to love this but it is not structurally sound. Save your $$
